title: DetectorsRefactored
description: Merged detector schemas with all detector types and common definitions

DetectorType.description: Type of detector for content analysis

DetectorCategory.description: High-level detector category for filtering and governance

SupportedAssetType.description: Canonical output asset type the detector can process

SourceAssetType.description: Source types where detector execution is supported

DetectorLifecycleStatus.description: Implementation lifecycle status of a detector

DetectorCatalogEntry.description: Catalog metadata for one detector type

categories.description: Detector categories used for routing, reporting, and policy

supported_asset_types.description: Asset modalities the detector can scan

recommended_model.description: Recommended model, engine, or package for this detector

DetectorCatalog.description: Detector capability catalog used for planning and runtime routing

default.notes: Detects confidential credentials like API keys, tokens, or passwords that could lead to security breaches.
Identifies personal data (e.g., names, emails, IDs) that must be protected for privacy and compliance.
Uses security rules to identify known malware patterns or suspicious file content.
Finds non-working or invalid links that reduce content quality and user trust.
Identifies vulnerabilities or insecure patterns in source code (e.g., hardcoded secrets).

User-defined rules and pipelines tailored to specific business needs. Supports regex, GLiNER2, AI/LLM (prompt-driven classification + extraction via a configured provider), text classification, image classification, feature extraction, and object detection pipelines.

SecretsEnabledPattern.description: Secrets detector pattern types. Each value maps to a detect-secrets plugin: artifactory=ArtifactoryDetector, aws=AWSKeyDetector, azure_storage=AzureStorageKeyDetector, basic_auth=BasicAuthDetector, cloudant=CloudantDetector, discord=DiscordBotTokenDetector, github=GitHubTokenDetector, gitlab=GitLabTokenDetector, high_entropy_base64=Base64HighEntropyString, high_entropy_hex=HexHighEntropyString, ibm_cloud_iam=IbmCloudIamDetector, ibm_cos_hmac=IbmCosHmacDetector, ip_public=IPPublicDetector, jwt=JwtTokenDetector, keyword=KeywordDetector, mailchimp=MailchimpDetector, npm=NpmDetector, openai=OpenAIDetector, private_key=PrivateKeyDetector, pypi=PypiTokenDetector, sendgrid=SendGridDetector, slack=SlackDetector, softlayer=SoftlayerDetector, square_oauth=SquareOAuthDetector, stripe=StripeDetector, telegram=TelegramBotTokenDetector, twilio=TwilioKeyDetector.

PIIEnabledPattern.description: Presidio entity types for PII detection. Global: CREDIT_CARD, CRYPTO, DATE_TIME, EMAIL_ADDRESS, IBAN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, NRP, LOCATION, PERSON, PHONE_NUMBER, MEDICAL_LICENSE, URL. USA: US_BANK_NUMBER, US_DRIVER_LICENSE, US_ITIN, US_PASSPORT, US_SSN. UK: UK_NHS. Spain: ES_NIF, ES_NIE. Italy: IT_FISCAL_CODE, IT_DRIVER_LICENSE, IT_VAR_CODE, IT_PASSPORT, IT_IDENTITY_CARD. Singapore: SG_NRIC_FIN, SG_UEN. Australia: AU_ABN, AU_ACN, AU_TFN, AU_MEDICARE. India: IN_PAN, IN_AADHAAR, IN_VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, IN_VOTER. Finland: FI_PERSONAL_IDENTITY_CODE. Poland: PL_PESEL. DACH: AT_SVNR, CH_AHV, DE_TAX_ID, EU_NATIONAL_ID.

PIIRecognizerPattern.description: Regex pattern entry for a custom Presidio recognizer

regex.description: Regular expression to match the entity

score.description: Confidence score assigned when this pattern matches (0-1)

PIICustomRecognizer.description: Ad-hoc Presidio recognizer added at runtime. Supports regex patterns, deny-list terms, or both. The recognizer is registered in the analyzer engine and applies to all scans with this config.

supported_entity.description: Entity label produced when this recognizer fires (e.g. MY_EMPLOYEE_ID)

supported_language.description: BCP-47 language code this recognizer applies to

deny_list.description: Exact-match deny-list terms for this recognizer

context.description: Context words that boost the score when found near a match (e.g. ['zip', 'code'])

SecretsDetectorConfig.title: SecretsDetectorConfig
SecretsDetectorConfig.description: Configuration for secrets detector powered by detect-secrets

enabled_patterns.description: Subset of detect-secrets plugins to enable. When null all supported plugins are active.

entropy_limit_base64.description: Entropy threshold for Base64HighEntropyString (0-8). Defaults to detect-secrets built-in of 4.5 when null. Lower values catch more secrets but increase false positives.

entropy_limit_hex.description: Entropy threshold for HexHighEntropyString (0-8). Defaults to detect-secrets built-in of 3.0 when null. Lower values catch more secrets but increase false positives.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum confidence score to report a finding (0-1)

max_findings.description: Maximum number of findings to return per asset

severity_threshold.description: Minimum severity level to include in results. Findings below this threshold are suppressed.

PIIDetectorConfig.title: PIIDetectorConfig
PIIDetectorConfig.description: Configuration for PII detector powered by Microsoft Presidio

enabled_patterns.description: Presidio entity types to detect. When null, all supported entities are enabled. Use PIIEnabledPattern values (e.g. EMAIL_ADDRESS, US_SSN, CREDIT_CARD).

severity_overrides.description: Per-entity severity overrides, keyed by Presidio entity type (e.g. {"CREDIT_CARD": "medium"}). Severity describes the label, not evidence quality: a recognizer that fires on OCR artifacts in one corpus may be genuinely critical in another. Overrides win over the built-in defaults.

language.description: BCP-47 language code for NER models (e.g. en, de, es)

spacy_model.description: spaCy model to load (e.g. en_core_web_sm, en_core_web_lg). Defaults to en_core_web_sm when null.

spacy_model_url.description: Wheel URL for the spaCy model. When set and the model is not installed, the CLI installs it at runtime.

custom_recognizers.description: Ad-hoc recognizers added to the Presidio registry at runtime. Each entry defines a regex-pattern or deny-list recognizer for a custom entity type.

max_length.description: Override spaCy's nlp.max_length (default 1,000,000 chars). Set higher than your longest expected input to avoid the E088 error. Prefer chunk_size for very large texts.

chunk_size.description: Split text into chunks of this many characters before analysis. Findings from all chunks are merged with corrected offsets. When null the full text is passed as-is (subject to max_length).

chunk_overlap.description: Character overlap between consecutive chunks. Helps detect entities that span a chunk boundary.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum Presidio confidence score to report a finding (0-1)

max_findings.description: Maximum number of findings to return per asset

YaraRuleConfig.title: YaraRuleConfig
YaraRuleConfig.description: A single YARA rule definition. Strings are assigned identifiers $s0, $s1, … in order and can be referenced in the condition by index or via 'any of them' / 'N of ($s*)'.

name.description: Rule identifier — letters, digits, and underscores only. Must be unique within a config.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the rule detects.

severity.description: Severity level emitted when the rule fires.

category.description: Free-form tag for grouping findings, e.g. 'secrets', 'malware', 'supply_chain'.

strings.description: YARA string patterns with optional modifiers. Assigned identifiers $s0, $s1, … in declaration order. Supported formats: regex (/pattern/ [ascii|wide|nocase|fullword]), literal ("text" [modifiers]), hex ({ HH HH … }).

condition.description: YARA condition expression, e.g. 'any of them', '2 of ($s*)', '$s0 and $s1'.

ThreatDetectorConfig.title: ThreatDetectorConfig
ThreatDetectorConfig.description: YARA-based threat detector. Compile one or more structured rule objects into a live YARA ruleset that is scanned against extracted asset content. Use the bundled examples as starting points and compose additional rules as needed.

rules.description: Rules to compile and run. When null or empty no scan is performed.

timeout.description: Maximum seconds to spend scanning a single asset before aborting.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum confidence score to report a finding (0-1). YARA confidence is computed from match count.

max_findings.description: Maximum number of findings to return per asset

severity_threshold.description: Minimum severity level to include in results. Findings below this threshold are suppressed.

max_findings.description: Maximum number of broken link findings to return per asset

CustomDetectorMethod.description: Execution method for custom detector logic

id.description: Stable ID for this regex rule

name.description: Display name for this regex rule

flags.description: Regex flags (for example i, m, s)

id.description: Stable ID for this keyword rule

name.description: Display name for this keyword rule

CustomExtractorField.description: One output field in the extraction schema

name.description: Output field name — becomes a key in extracted_data JSON

description.description: Human-readable hint for what this field captures

entity_label.description: GLiNER2 schema label used for extraction (ENTITY and CLASSIFIER methods)

regex_pattern.description: Regex with one named capture group (?P<value>...) for RULESET method

min_confidence.description: Minimum GLiNER confidence for this field

required.description: If true, skip saving extraction when this field is empty

CustomExtractorConfig.description: Structured extraction — runs after detection fires on the same content

content_limit.description: Chars of content to pass to extractor (classifier matched_content is only 320 chars)

entity_descriptions.description: Optional GLiNER2 schema descriptions keyed by entity label

custom_detector_key.description: Stable key used to identify one custom detector instance

extractor.description: Optional structured extraction — runs when detector fires

max_findings.description: Maximum number of findings to return per asset

CodeSecurityDetectorConfig.title: CodeSecurityDetectorConfig
CodeSecurityDetectorConfig.description: Configuration for the code security detector powered by Bandit static analysis. Use skips/tests to control which Bandit checks run.

skips.description: Bandit test IDs to skip (e.g. ['B101', 'B105']). When null all tests run. Takes precedence over tests when both are set.

tests.description: Explicit list of Bandit test IDs to run (e.g. ['B102', 'B301']). When null all tests (minus skips) run.

severity_threshold.description: Minimum Bandit issue severity to report. Findings below this threshold are suppressed.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum confidence score to report a finding (0-1)

max_findings.description: Maximum number of findings to return per asset

GenericDetectorConfig.title: GenericDetectorConfig
GenericDetectorConfig.description: Generic config for detectors without specialised parameters.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum confidence score to report a finding (0-1)

max_findings.description: Maximum number of findings to return per asset

flags.description: Legacy integer flags (e.g. re.IGNORECASE). Prefer the boolean fields below instead.

severity.description: Severity level assigned to findings from this pattern. When omitted, defaults to high (confidence is always 1.0 for regex).

case_sensitive.description: Whether matching is case-sensitive. Set to false for case-insensitive matching.

dot_nl.description: Whether the dot (.) metacharacter matches newline characters.

literal.description: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression.

longest_match.description: Prefer the longest possible match instead of the first match (RE2 only).

max_mem.description: Maximum memory (bytes) for the RE2 automaton. RE2 only; ignored with stdlib fallback.

group.description: Capture group index to extract as matched content. 0 = entire match (default).

PipelineSeverityRule.title: PipelineSeverityRule
PipelineSeverityRule.description: Maps a predicted label to a severity level. Pattern is matched case-insensitively as a substring of the label.

pattern.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against the predicted label (e.g. 'spam', 'nsfw', 'person').

LLMLabelDefinition.title: LLMLabelDefinition
LLMLabelDefinition.description: One classification label the AI detector may assign to content.

name.description: Label name returned by the model (e.g. 'good', 'bad', 'violent').

description.description: Guidance describing when this label applies.

LLMOutputField.title: LLMOutputField
LLMOutputField.description: One structured property the AI detector extracts and stores in finding metadata and extracted_data.

name.description: Output field name — becomes a key in extracted_data JSON.

description.description: Hint for what this field captures.

LLMProviderRuntime.title: LLMProviderRuntime
LLMProviderRuntime.description: Runtime-only provider credentials injected by the API at dispatch time. Never persisted with the detector config and rejected on create/update.

model.description: Resolved model identifier (e.g. gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4-5, gemini-2.0-flash).

base_url.description: Base URL for OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Null for managed providers.

context_size.description: Optional context window size configured for the provider.

supports_vision.description: Whether the resolved provider/model accepts image/PDF input. When true the detector renders supported files to images and sends them to the model as multimodal input instead of extracting text.

LLMPipelineSchema.title: LLMPipelineSchema
LLMPipelineSchema.description: AI detector pipeline. Sends content to a configured LLM provider with a system prompt, classifies it against a label set, and extracts structured fields. Predicted labels become findings (severity via severity_map); extracted fields are stored in finding metadata and extracted_data.

system_prompt.description: Instruction describing what the model should detect, classify, and extract.

response_example.description: Optional few-shot example of the JSON the model should return.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Lower is more deterministic.

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens to generate. Provider default when null.

labels.description: Classification taxonomy the model assigns to content.

multi_label.description: Allow more than one label per asset.

severity.description: Default severity when no severity_map rule matches a predicted label.

severity_map.description: Ordered rules mapping predicted labels to severity levels. First matching rule wins.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum model confidence to report a label as a finding (0-1).

output_fields.description: Structured properties the model extracts. Stored in finding metadata and extracted_data.

content_limit.description: Maximum characters of content sent to the model.

provider_runtime.description: Runtime-only credentials injected by the API at dispatch. Never persisted; rejected on create/update.

TextClassificationPipelineSchema.title: TextClassificationPipelineSchema
TextClassificationPipelineSchema.description: Text classification pipeline using a HuggingFace fine-tuned model. Runs a single model; create multiple custom detectors to run multiple classifiers.

model.description: HuggingFace hub ID (e.g. 'mrm8488/bert-tiny-finetuned-sms-spam-detection') or absolute local directory path.

model_revision.description: Git branch, tag, or commit hash when fetching from the HuggingFace hub.

device.description: Inference device: 'cpu' (default), 'cuda', 'mps', or a CUDA device string like 'cuda:0'.

top_k.description: Maximum number of top predictions to return. When null all labels above confidence_threshold are returned.

function_to_apply.description: Score normalization: 'softmax' for single-label, 'sigmoid' for multi-label, 'none' for raw logits.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum prediction confidence to report a label as a finding (0-1).

severity.description: Default severity when no severity_map rule matches.

severity_map.description: Ordered rules mapping predicted labels to severity levels. First matching rule wins.

max_length.description: Override the tokenizer's maximum sequence length.

chunk_size.description: Split text into chunks of this many characters before classification.

ImageClassificationPipelineSchema.title: ImageClassificationPipelineSchema
ImageClassificationPipelineSchema.description: Image classification pipeline using a HuggingFace vision model. Runs a single model; create multiple custom detectors to run multiple classifiers.

model.description: HuggingFace hub ID or local path. Defaults to 'google/vit-base-patch16-224' when null.

model_revision.description: Git branch, tag, or commit hash when fetching from the HuggingFace hub.

device.description: Inference device: 'cpu' (default), 'cuda', 'mps', or a CUDA device string like 'cuda:0'.

top_k.description: Maximum number of top predictions to return per image.

function_to_apply.description: Score normalization applied after the model forward pass.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum prediction confidence to report a label as a finding (0-1). Defaults to 0 so all top_k predictions are reported.

severity_map.description: Ordered rules mapping predicted labels to severity levels. Labels with no matching rule receive 'info' severity.

ObjectDetectionPipelineSchema.title: ObjectDetectionPipelineSchema
ObjectDetectionPipelineSchema.description: Object detection pipeline using a HuggingFace model. Runs a single detector; create multiple custom detectors to run multiple detection models.

model.description: HuggingFace hub ID (e.g. 'facebook/detr-resnet-50', 'hustvl/yolos-small') or absolute local directory path.

model_revision.description: Git branch, tag, or commit hash when fetching from the HuggingFace hub.

device.description: Inference device: 'cpu' (default), 'cuda', 'mps', or a CUDA device string like 'cuda:0'.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum detection confidence to report an object as a finding (0-1).

top_k.description: Keep only the top-k highest-confidence detections per image.

nms_threshold.description: IoU threshold for non-maximum suppression. When null the model's default post-processing is used.

min_box_area.description: Minimum bounding-box area in pixels (width × height). Smaller detections are suppressed.

severity_map.description: Ordered rules mapping detected object labels to severity levels. Labels with no matching rule receive 'info' severity.
